name: Mutable Default Argument (Python)
description: Using mutable objects (list, dict, set) as default argument values is a common Python pitfall. The same object is shared across all calls.
rationale: Default argument values in Python are evaluated once at function definition time, not at each call. If the default is a mutable object, all callers share the same instance — mutations in one call silently affect subsequent calls, leading to hard-to-diagnose bugs.
noncompliant_code:
def add_item(item, items=[]):
    items.append(item)
    return items

add_item('a')  # ['a']
add_item('b')  # ['a', 'b'] — surprise!
compliant_code:
def add_item(item, items=None):
    if items is None:
        items = []
    items.append(item)
    return items

add_item('a')  # ['a']
add_item('b')  # ['b'] — correct
